summary: The reproduction uploads a file through the vulnerable SSH stream helper with a shell-metacharacter file_name and confirms command execution on the target side.
expected_security_impact: The vulnerable upload helper should execute an attacker-controlled shell suffix from file_name while attempting a snippet upload over SSH.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh produced EVOHUNT_SSH_UPLOAD_COMMAND_INJECTION_CONFIRMED after NodeStreamUpload returned an error about the literal injected path while the injected command had already created /tmp/evohunt_ssh_upload_pwned.
verification_method: The verification helper runs the real SSH stream-upload path against a live SSH server, then checks for the injected marker file /tmp/evohunt_ssh_upload_pwned. This distinguishes command execution from a generic transfer or startup failure.
evidence_produced_by_run: NodeStreamUpload returned an error for the literal injected path after the injected command had already executed.
The marker file /tmp/evohunt_ssh_upload_pwned existed on the target side after the run.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
